coredata.dc:title: Plant biomass-derived nanomaterials for biosensing and bioimaging applications

coredata.dc:description: Nanotechnology has revolutionized the biomedical frontier by offering innovative diagnostic and therapeutic solutions to existing or emerging diseases. However, the physicochemical synthesis of nanostructures demands an eco-friendly, cost-effective, and sustainable way of nanomaterial synthesis. Plant biomass-derived nanomaterials are more biocompatible, eco-friendly and promote sustainability as these harness the natural capabilities of plants to act as reducing and stabilizing agents. This review prospects different plant biomass-based nanomaterials synthesized utilizing plant-derived precursors to minimize environmental impact. In biosensing, these nanostructures have proven their value and exhibited high performance equivalent to the physiochemically synthesized nanomaterials for health and environmental monitoring. Their crucial role in therapeutic applications and diverse bioimaging modalities like fluorescence, magnetic resonance, photoacoustic, and computed tomography have further endorsed their potential. Integration into theragnostics and multimodal imaging reveals fresh avenues for personalized medicine. This review provides a comprehensive summary of recent advancements of plant-derived nanomaterials for diagnostics and therapeutics. It also discusses the associated challenges of scalability, reproducibility, and stability that acts as a propelling force to conduct further research and unlock the full potential of these nanomaterials.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Different types of plant biomass utilized for synthesizing nanomaterials.
Methods for the synthesis of nanomaterials.

Diverse uses of biomass-derived NPs in biosensing. A. Electrochemical immunosensor for the detection of lung cancer biomarkers by AuNPs synthesized using Mangifera indica leaves extract B. Fluorescence based turn on aptasensor utlizing FRET mechanism for the detection of S. aureus employing AuNPs and CQDs derived from olive leaves C. Fluorometric detection of imidacloprid by utilizing CQDs from WS (Wall nut shells) D. Fluorometric detection of fluoride ions by T-CQDs/Eu³ ⁺ nanoprobe synthesized from taro peels.
Use of plant biomass-derived NPs in different bioimaging.

Applications of Plant-derived nanomaterials (PDNMs): In Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), PDNMs enhance contrast in MRI scans for improved visualization and imaging. In fluorescence imaging, PDNMs conjugated with fluorescent probes are used to enable high-resolution optical imaging. In computed tomography (CT) Imaging, PDNMs act as contrasting agents in CT-scans and X-rays to enhance CT imaging. In theragnostics, PDNMs facilitate a combination of imaging and therapy, such as drug delivery and real-time monitoring. In photoacoustic imaging, PDNMs improve ultrasound-guided optical imaging for deeper tissue visualization through photoacoustic imaging, and in environmental Diagnosis, PDNMs can be used in biosensors for detecting bacterial/fungal spores, environmental toxins or biomarkers. (Image created using Biorender).
Synthesis methods and characteristics of plant biomass-derived nanomaterials.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Biosensing applications of various plant biomass-derived nanomaterials.

row.entry: Optical fiber biosensor utilizing localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) of AuNPs; detects refractive index changes from antigen-antibody binding, measured via intensity shift and wavelength shift.
Plant derived natural polyphenol morin hydrate
Spectroscopic sensing through UV-Visible, fluorescence, and circular dichroism spectroscopy; signal enhancement attributed to the charged nature and surface activity of γ-irradiated NPs.
0.066 U/mL in standard conditions, improved to 0.029–0.157 U/mL in the presence of physiological entities like glucose, cholesterol, and insulin, indicating high sensitivity and specificity.

The electrochemical sensor utilizes ZnO-modified Glassy Carbon Electrodes (GCE), where dopamine's redox reactions (2-electron/2-proton transfer) induce changes in current, analysed via techniques like cyclic voltammetry and square wave voltammetry
Carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA) and v-YES1 Yamaguchi sarcoma viral oncogene homolog 1 (YES1)
Electrochemical immunosensor based on a sandwich-type assay using antibody functionalised AuNPs as electroactive probes
0.0034 ng/mL for CEA and 0.0022 ng/mL for YES1
Voltammetry-based immunosensor on an interdigitated electrode (IDE) using CCP-IONP immobilization, where increased current signals indicate specific anti-CCP binding
Prostate-specific antigen (PSA), a biomarker for prostate cancer

A label-free electrochemical immunosensor based on impedance changes proportional to PSA concentration, facilitated by the enhanced conductivity and stability provided by the mucilage-AuNPs-AgNPs composite
The peroxidase-like activity of AgNPs catalyzes the oxidation of o-phenylenediamine (OPD) to a colored product (DAP), which is inhibited by cysteine due to its thiol-induced interaction with the NPs.
Label-free non-faradaic electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (nf-EIS) measuring impedance changes due to bacterial cell trapping and membrane disruption by AgNPs
Surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy leveraging electromagnetic and chemical enhancement mechanisms due to Ag/CDs interaction with thiram molecules
Electrochemical sensing using a NiFe₂O₄-modified glassy carbon electrode, exploiting catalytic activity and high conductivity for PCP detection
Dual-mode electrochemical sensor using molecularly imprinted polymer and AuNPs/BC-modified glassy carbon electrode for DPV and EIS
Electrochemical sensor using EMBPC@ZrO2 composite-modified glassy carbon electrode; EMBPC enhances conductivity and surface area, while ZrO2 adsorbs MP via affinity toward phosphate groups
The fluorescence emission of WS-CQDs enhances upon binding with imidacloprid, forming a ground-state complex and exhibiting a blue-shift in the emission wavelength (441–436 nm)

The magnetomotor exhibits peroxidase-like activity, where the presence of glyphosate inhibits the catalytic activity due to the binding of its phosphate group with Zr-O clusters, leading to a colorimetric change in TMB oxidation
Fluorescence quenching of CQDs by Eu3+ ions, followed by fluorescence regeneration upon fluoride ion addition
Dual-emission ratiometric fluorescence sensing, where the fluorescence intensity ratio changes with the presence of Cd²⁺
Colorimetric sensing through ligand-metal complex formation, causing AgNPs-CQDs aggregation and visible discoloration from brownish-yellow to colorless
Fluorescence quenching via dynamic quenching for Hg²⁺ and a combination of the inner filter effect and dynamic quenching for Cr⁶⁺
0.018 µM for both Hg²⁺ and Cr⁶⁺
Surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) enabled by hot spots and oxidation-resistant nanoparticle surfaces
4-mercaptobenzoic acid (1 nM) and Sudan I (0.126 ppm)
Fluorescence quenching via chelation between Fe³ ⁺ ions and oxygen-containing functional groups of CDs; energy transfer to Fe³ ⁺ d-orbitals

Electrochemical sensor using GNDPC@β-CD modified glassy carbon electrode (GCE); the 3D honeycomb porous structure enhances conductivity and adsorption, while β-CD improves molecular recognition and accumulation
Electrochemical sensing using DPV; the 2D-AC enhances adsorption and electron transfer for ROX reduction at −0.66 V
Natural soapnut solution used as a reducing agent
Electrochemical detection using DPV, Detection relies on changes in electron transfer due to DNA immobilization and hybridization with target miRNA
Electrochemical label-free aptasensor; the detection relied on changes in electron transfer resistance and current caused by AFP binding to immobilized aptamers, monitored through EIS and DPV.
N-mCg-based aptasensor achieved LODs of 60.8 fg·mL⁻¹ (DPV) and 61.8 fg·mL⁻¹ (EIS).
Electrochemical sensing via electrooxidation of sialic acid from α1GP, which hinders interfacial electron transfer of [Fe(CN)₆]³ ⁻/⁴⁻ redox probe
Electrochemical sensing: dopamine oxidation at the 3D porous structure of the LIG-based electrode, generating measurable current signals

Lactic acid is oxidized by lactate oxidase near the gate electrode, producing H₂O₂. The H₂O₂ undergoes electrochemical catalysis on the CeO₂ NPs-modified electrode, altering the gate potential and channel current of the solution-gated graphene transistor

Electrochemical detection via a glassy carbon electrode, utilizing the high conductivity and adsorption properties of PCS along with the molecular recognition ability of β-CD to enhance MP binding and signal response.
Fluorescence "on-off-on" sensing via inner filter effect (IFE), where AuNPs quench N-CDs fluorescence and thiram-induced AuNPs aggregation restores fluorescence
Surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) with machine learning (ML) for classification
"turn-off" due to electron transfer from CQDs to metal ions, and "turn-on" with ascorbic acid for Fe³ ⁺ detection
Fe³ ⁺ (0.69 µM), Cu²⁺ (0.99 µM), Hg²⁺ (0.25 µM), and AA detection via Fe³ ⁺-CQDs at 4.6 µM
Heavy metal ions (Pb²⁺, Cd²⁺, Zn²⁺, Cu²⁺, Mg²⁺)
Capacitive deionization using electrosorption, enhanced by Fe₃O₄ NPs improving surface charge, wettability, and adsorption site
